coordinates.question_shape: vendor and model for an identified system
coordinates.evidence_shape: The compile preserved an entity-labeled source text row containing vendor and model text, but QA planned unsupported text membership and did not query the labeled source text directly.
coordinates.do_not_promote: Do not add a broad same-row join from this coordinate alone.

coordinates.question_shape: person identified by role at an institution
coordinates.evidence_shape: The answer-bearing row is a correspondence/signature role row, while the planned query anchored on a different party row and the compile did not expose a reusable person-role surface for the registrar role.
coordinates.do_not_promote: Do not hard-code registrar, institution names, or local section labels.

coordinates.question_shape: which section contains a named chronology
coordinates.evidence_shape: The compile/query surface indexed earlier source sections but did not preserve the answer-bearing section as a queryable section coordinate.
coordinates.do_not_promote: Do not teach a specific section title; preserve all source headings that carry retrievable roles.

coordinates.question_shape: which section sets out the basis for possession or custody
coordinates.evidence_shape: The compile exposes possession/custody state but not the source section that explains the basis, nor the corroborating source section as addressable evidence.
coordinates.do_not_promote: Do not collapse basis evidence into custody state; keep state and source-basis addressability separate.

coordinates.question_shape: object title and year for an identified item
coordinates.evidence_shape: A direct title-looking predicate refers to a legal/ownership title, while the object title and year live in a source-row description and same-row numeric token.
coordinates.do_not_promote: Do not promote broad row-context joins; distinguish object-title questions from legal-title predicates.
